etymology.description: Depicts four dots, perhaps three grains of sand -> indicates "a few, a little" -> phonetically borrowed from 小(小) (xiǎo, "small")
etymology.interpretation: The character 少(少) (shǎo, "few") originally depicted four dots, possibly representing grains of sand, conveying the impression of a small quantity. It was phonetically borrowed from 小(小) (xiǎo, "small"), which uses three dots to signify smallness, reinforcing the sense of 'few' through the idea of minimal amounts.

examples.chinese: 这本书有很少的字(這本書有很少的字)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū yǒu hěn shǎo de zì.
examples.english: This book has very few words.

examples.chinese: 来参加演出的人很少(來參加演出的人很少)。
examples.pinyin: Lái cānjiā yǎnchū de rén hěn shǎo.
examples.english: Few people came to attend the performance.

examples.chinese: 这个季节雨水很少(這個季節雨水很少)。
examples.english: There is very little rain this season.
